title: festuca altaica var. scabrella
intro: Festuca altaica var. scabrella, also known as Tibetan fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. This plant has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Festuca altaica var. scabrella include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. In traditional Tibetan medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in combination with other herbs to create infusions and decoctions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
